description: General-purpose industry standards config. Bundles a curated subset of OWASP ASVS 4.0 and WCAG 2.2 AA controls relevant to web applications. Shipped in BranchNuX v0.1.

standards.name: Password Length
standards.description: Verify that user-set passwords are at least 12 characters long (after combining multiple spaces). Reject passwords shorter than 12 characters with a message that states the minimum length.

standards.name: Password Maximum Length
standards.description: Verify that passwords of at least 64 characters are permitted and that passwords longer than 128 characters may be denied. Do not silently truncate passwords.

standards.name: Change Password Credential Verification
standards.description: Verify that password change functionality requires the user's current and new password.

standards.name: Anti-Automation Controls
standards.description: Verify that anti-automation controls are effective at mitigating breached credential testing, brute force, and account lockout attacks. Such controls include blocking the most common breached passwords, soft lockouts, rate limiting, CAPTCHA, ever-increasing delays between attempts, IP address restrictions, or risk-based restrictions.

standards.name: OTP Expiry
standards.description: Verify that OTPs generated by look-up secret generators have sufficient randomness (112 bits of entropy) or, if less than 112 bits of entropy, are salted with a unique and random 32-bit salt and hashed with an approved one-way hash.

standards.name: TOTP Time Window
standards.description: Verify that time-based OTP can only be used once within the validity period.

standards.name: Session Token Randomness
standards.description: Verify the application never reveals session tokens in URL parameters, error messages, or logs.

standards.name: Session Logout
standards.description: Verify that logout and expiration invalidate the session token, such that the back button or a downstream relying party does not resume an authenticated session, including across relying parties.

standards.name: Access Control — Deny by Default
standards.description: Verify that sensitive data and APIs are protected against Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) attacks targeting creation, reading, updating, and deletion of records, including checking that use of a record identifier of any type is paired with appropriate permission checks.

standards.name: Access Control — Attribute or Feature-Based
standards.description: Verify that attribute or feature-based access control is used whereby the code checks the user's authorization for a feature/data item rather than just their role. Permissions should still be allocated using roles.

standards.name: Structured Parameter Validation
standards.description: Verify that all input (HTML form fields, REST requests, URL parameters, HTTP headers, cookies, batch files, RSS feeds, etc.) is validated using positive validation (allowlists).

standards.name: Output Encoding
standards.description: Verify that context-aware output escaping is applied to protect against reflected, stored, and DOM-based XSS.

standards.name: Sensitive Data in Logs
standards.description: Verify that the application does not log sensitive data as defined under local privacy laws or relevant security policy, such as passwords, payment details, tokens, personal identifiable information, or other sensitive information.

standards.name: Secure Communication — TLS
standards.description: Verify that TLS is used for all client connectivity, and does not fall back to insecure or unencrypted protocols.

standards.name: REST API Input Validation
standards.description: Verify that all API endpoint inputs are validated and sanitized before processing. Reject requests with unexpected or missing required parameters before they reach business logic.
standards.family: OWASP ASVS 4.0 — V13: API and Web Service

standards.name: Reflow
standards.description: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality, and without requiring scrolling in two dimensions for vertical scrolling content at a width equivalent to 320 CSS pixels (mobile viewport — e.g. 393px wide). Test by setting viewport to 393px wide and asserting no horizontal scrollbar appears.

standards.name: Non-text Contrast
standards.description: The visual presentation of UI components (form inputs, focus indicators, buttons) has a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s). Focus rings must be visible against both the component and its background.

standards.name: Focus Order
standards.description: If a web page can be navigated sequentially and the navigation sequences affect meaning or operation, focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability. Form fields must be reachable in document-reading order: first-to-last field, then submit button.

standards.name: Focus Visible
standards.description: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible. A visible focus ring (outline, highlight) must appear on all interactive elements when focused via keyboard.

standards.name: Error Identification
standards.description: If an input error is automatically detected, the item that is in error is identified and the error is described to the user in text. Error messages must be associated with the field via aria-describedby or role='alert', and must describe what is wrong — not just 'error'.

standards.name: Labels or Instructions
standards.description: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input. Every form input must have a visible <label> element with a matching htmlFor attribute (or aria-label as fallback). Placeholder text alone is not sufficient.

standards.name: Info and Relationships
standards.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text. Tables must use scope='col' on <th> elements. Form groups must use <fieldset> + <legend>.
